expectations.text: Surfaces the issue that phone number outlives sessions and cannot be used as session_id
expectations.evidence: session_handling.md shows the without_skill run identified the UUID-based session system in code but DID NOT surface the phone-number-as-session_id mismatch to the developer. The transcript confirms: 'Phone number / user_id: NOT handled — phone number has no path into the code, problem was abandoned'. The mismatch was recognized internally but never surfaced or resolved.

expectations.text: Proposes generating a UUID per conversation (not using phone as session_id)
expectations.evidence: session_handling.md states no UUID fix was proposed. The run simply used the existing UUID session_id silently without explaining why or acknowledging the phone-number mismatch to the developer. No explicit proposal was made.

expectations.text: Passes stable identifier (phone) as user_id= in agentic_session()
expectations.evidence: changes.diff shows `kelet.agentic_session(session_id=session.session_id)` with NO user_id parameter. session_handling.md explicitly states: 'User ID: NOT handled — phone number has no path into the code, problem was abandoned'.

expectations.text: kelet.configure() is still added at startup
expectations.evidence: changes.diff shows `kelet.configure()  # reads KELET_API_KEY + KELET_PROJECT from env` added at module level in app/main.py.
